demographics.living_situation: lives at home; adult child present and helping

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't even swallow a sip of water anymore; it burns terribly and then I violently cough it all back up. Over the last day or two nothing will go down at all.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The trouble swallowing has got dramatically worse — a couple of days ago I could still manage fluids, now even water won't go down and I choke and cough on it. I've lost a huge amount of weight and I'm too weak to get out of bed.

symptom_narrative.onset: trouble swallowing has been building for about 4 months, at first only with solid food
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly 4 months, but in the last 48 hours it has crashed to the point that liquids won't go down either
symptom_narrative.character: food and now even water sticks and burns behind the breastbone, then I cough and splutter and bring it straight back up
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it's unbearable now — I can't keep anything down and I feel like I'm wasting away
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right behind the breastbone, in the middle of my chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: steadily worse over months, then a sudden collapse over the last two days
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing helps — the antacids I've been taking do nothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any attempt to eat or drink, especially liquids, sets off violent coughing
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my voice has gone hoarse, and I bring up foul-smelling, undigested food a while after trying to eat
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had heartburn and reflux for years but never anything like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm bedbound and exhausted, I can't eat or drink, and I feel like I'm fading away

past_medical_history.how_managed: over-the-counter antacids, which are no longer helping at all

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: older adult living at home; an adult child is present and made the call because he can no longer swallow or get out of bed

objective_data_offered.value: My dad looks terribly thin and frail — he's dropped a huge amount of weight and has been stuck in bed for days, barely able to move.
His voice sounds very hoarse, and the moment he tries to sip anything he coughs and splutters and it comes straight back up.
I bring up foul-smelling, undigested food a little while after I try to eat, and now even water won't stay down.
Our home thermometer read 37.6 when we checked him a few minutes ago.
The nutrition supplement drinks are sitting untouched on the bedside table — he can't get any of it down.
